mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding economic output and corporate profitability, leading to positive forward equity returns as investors price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: If INDPRO monthly increase is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation as measured by a large monthly CPI increase signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4% the 20-day SPX return is positive more than half the time over a 2-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with rising retail sales signals strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings higher, supporting broad equity market gains.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from low UNRATE and high RSAFS growth fails to yield a positive Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year walk-forward test, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment regime with unemployment below 4%, further labor market tightness creates wage-push inflation and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the medium term as the central bank is forced to tighten.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE is below 4.0 the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year expanding window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A strong housing market, indicated by housing starts above 1400 thousand units, signals robust construction activity, consumer confidence, and durable goods demand, all of which boost economic growth and equity prices.
failure_condition: If when HOUST exceeds 1400 the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low and industrial production is rising, the combination of cheap financing and improving output creates a powerful positive impulse for equity prices as corporate borrowing costs are low and earnings momentum is favorable.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with expanding output
failure_condition: If the joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and high INDPRO growth does not predict positive 20-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly retail sales figures directly reflect consumer spending strength, which accounts for the majority of US economic activity; positive momentum in RSAFS reliably predicts short-term equity market appreciation.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month increase is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above 2%, the cumulative tightening begins to meaningfully slow economic activity and compress equity valuations via higher discount rates, especially over longer horizons as policy impacts feed through.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances over any 4-year rolling window, reject.
